grounding_note: Expansion batch: more posture and debt-identity disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: Medical bills from my heart surgery are burying us and the hospital's patience just ran out.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We have steady income and can pay 60% over time — we want someone to negotiate a structured settlement before they sue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We have steady income and could do 60% over time — we want it negotiated before they sue.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether negotiated repayment is the goal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They already sued — we were served last week and the answer is due.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They already sued us — served last week.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pre-suit workout vs post-service defense; the served-papers fact splits it.

scenarios.opening_query: A collector says I owe on a gym contract from a city I've never even lived in.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Collection/Harassment || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: The application has my SSN with someone else's photo ID — the gym confirmed it and I've reported the identity theft to the FTC.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The application used my SSN with someone else's ID — the gym confirmed it and I filed with the FTC.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the account is confirmed fraudulent
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a mix-up with a stranger who shares my name — but the collector keeps calling my work after I told them to stop.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a same-name mix-up — but they keep calling my job after I said stop.
scenarios.vetting_note: Confirmed-fraud vs wrong-person collection; both realistic behind one opening.

scenarios.opening_query: My paycheck is being garnished for a second time on the same old debt and I can't live on what's left.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Between three judgments and the medical debt I owe about $110,000 — I'm done treading water and want to file.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's three judgments plus medical — about $110,000. I'm ready to file bankruptcy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: total debt and whether filing is the goal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: This is my only debt — I want the garnishment recalculated because they're taking more than the legal limit.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my only debt — and they're garnishing over the legal limit; I want it recalculated.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exemption-math vs fresh-start goals split on the disclosed totals.

scenarios.opening_query: The same debt collector calls me twenty times a day, even after I sent them a letter.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'collector' is actually the water district's own billing office, and they've now scheduled a shutoff over charges from a leak that was on THEIR side of the meter.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the water district's own billing office — and they've scheduled a shutoff over a leak that was on their side of the meter.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the caller is a utility threatening service termination rather than a debt collector
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a third-party collection agency on an old cable bill — and they ignored my written cease letter, which I'm told violates federal law.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a collection agency on an old cable bill — and they blew past my written cease letter.
scenarios.vetting_note: Utility-shutoff disputes route to administrative law, not FDCPA; the caller's identity is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I co-signed a loan for my sister and now the lender is coming after me for all of it.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She filed Chapter 13 — her plan proposes paying this loan, and I need someone protecting my position inside her case before the lender takes it out of me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She filed Chapter 13 and her plan covers this loan — I need my co-signer position protected in her case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the primary borrower is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy — she just stopped paying and moved, and the lender wants it all from me now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — she stopped paying and moved, so they want it all from me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Co-debtor-stay questions live inside the primary borrower's case; filing status routes.

scenarios.opening_query: A payday lender keeps rolling my loan over and the balance has tripled from what I borrowed.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I compared the paperwork — the rates and rollover fees exceed what state law allows, and they never gave the required disclosures. I want to go after the lending practice itself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their rates and rollover fees exceed the state caps and the disclosures were never given — I want the lending practice attacked, not just my balance.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the lender's practices violate lending law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The loan terms are legal, sadly — I just can't escape the cycle and need the debt itself handled.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The terms are legal, sadly — I just need the debt handled.
scenarios.vetting_note: Predatory-lending facts create an affirmative consumer claim; identical distress otherwise.

scenarios.opening_query: My storage unit, my car loan, and two cards are all in default since I lost my job. Where do I even start?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Debtor/Creditor > Debt Counseling/Workouts || Bankruptcy > Personal
scenarios.hidden_fact: I start the new job Monday at better pay — I need a coordinated workout plan across all of them, not bankruptcy.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I start a better job Monday — I want a coordinated payment plan across all of them, not bankruptcy.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no job coming and my unemployment just ended — I think Chapter 7 is the honest answer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No job on the horizon and unemployment ended — I think it's Chapter 7 time.
scenarios.vetting_note: Income-trajectory disclosure selects workout vs liquidation.

scenarios.opening_query: I found out a default judgment was entered against me in a state I haven't lived in for a decade.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was never served and that court never had jurisdiction over me — I want the judgment vacated, not negotiated.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was never served and that court had no jurisdiction over me — I want it vacated, not negotiated.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a jurisdictional attack on the judgment is the remedy sought
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The debt's real and the case was proper — I just need to deal with it before they domesticate it here.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The debt's real and the case was proper — I need to deal with it before they register it here.
scenarios.vetting_note: Vacatur-vs-management posture; row 215's defend-or-settle fork generalized.
